metlife.name: MetLife Stadium
metlife.surfaceType: hybrid grass (temporary, laid over turf base)
metlife.tacticalBias: can slow sharp passing sequences while rewarding compact, reactive defending
knownIssues: players reported softness and slip concerns during early tests
ball-speed inconsistency was a recurring criticism in venue coverage
temporary grass adaptation over an NFL venue raised fairness questions
metlife.sourceNotes: Venue criticism summarized from NY Post and broader media coverage of 2026 World Cup grass concerns.

attStadium.name: AT&T Stadium
attStadium.surfaceType: modular hybrid grass tray over enclosed NFL venue base
attStadium.tacticalBias: more predictable in climate control, but still sensitive to root establishment and firmness variation
knownIssues: temporary grass transition creates questions about seam consistency
enclosed venue conditions can preserve moisture differently than open-air stadiums
surface adaptation period may affect acceleration and plant foot confidence
attStadium.sourceNotes: Modeled from reporting on temporary grass logistics in NFL-hosted World Cup venues.

lumenField.name: Lumen Field
lumenField.surfaceType: temporary natural-hybrid grass installed for tournament use
lumenField.tacticalBias: weather and moisture can dampen roll speed, favoring direct play if footing becomes uncertain
knownIssues: weather exposure can change softness and traction across match windows
players may feel uneven confidence between planted turns and straight-line running
temporary grass over a non-native venue base can affect ball-roll predictability
lumenField.sourceNotes: Static venue profile informed by temporary-grass concerns and open-air surface management dynamics.
